title: Eggs in Purgatory (Uova in Purgatorio)

overview.description: Eggs gently poached in a rich garlic and tomato sauce with basil, chilli and Parmesan.

method: Heat the olive oil over a medium heat.
Cook the sliced garlic gently until fragrant but not browned.
Add the chilli flakes and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Simmer gently for 15 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Tear in most of the basil.
Make four small wells in the sauce.
Crack an egg into each well.
Cover with a lid and cook for 4–6 minutes until the whites are set but the yolks remain runny.
Scatter over the Parmesan and remaining basil.

learning.did-you-know: The bubbling red tomato sauce is said to resemble the flames of purgatory, giving the dish its memorable name.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a simple Italian tomato sauce and gently poach eggs directly in it.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, comforting meal made almost entirely from pantry staples, offering an Italian counterpart to Shakshuka.
chapman-tips: Keep the garlic pale—burnt garlic will make the whole dish bitter.
A lid helps the egg whites cook before the yolks overcook.
Finish with your best extra virgin olive oil for extra flavour.
Good bread is almost as important as the eggs.
notes.batch-cooking: The tomato sauce freezes beautifully. Defrost, reheat and add fresh eggs when serving.
notes.leftovers: The sauce makes an excellent pasta sauce the following day.
